edge_hypothesis: RSI pullback tail harvesters achieved PF=1.71 (161 trades, all-regime ACCEPT) using RSI<40 as the pullback threshold. But the advisory flagged a critical insight: the 'shallow dip' variant (rsi_shallow_dip_tail_7to1, only 38 trades) had borderline overfit issues. The OPPOSITE approach — DEEPER dips — has never been tested. The hypothesis: in a confirmed EMA_200 uptrend, an RSI_14 reading below 30 (deeply oversold) signals a liquidation cascade or panic sell-off that has overshot the trend. These events are structurally mean-reverting because: (1) leveraged long positions get liquidated, creating mechanical selling pressure that exhausts itself, (2) the underlying uptrend (close > EMA_200, EMA_50 slope up) is intact, meaning the selling is temporary dislocation, not trend reversal. By requiring RSI<30 instead of RSI<40, we reduce trade count significantly (~50%) but each entry occurs at extreme price displacement from the trend EMA — meaning the DISTANCE back to fair value is larger, increasing average winner size. At 8:1 and 9:1 R:R, only 15% and 12% win rates needed respectively. The deep-dip entries should have HIGHER win rates than standard RSI<40 entries because the mean-reversion force is stronger at extremes. We also test EMA_50 as the trend EMA (instead of EMA_200) for faster trend detection — this captures shorter-duration trends but may introduce whipsaw. Regime assumption: TRENDING primarily (pullbacks presuppose a trend), with some ranging edges (range-bound oscillation can produce RSI<30 at support). Invalidation: if trade count drops below 40, the deep RSI filter is too restrictive for actionable signal generation on 1h.

variants.name: rsi_deep_pullback_1h_8to1
variants.description: RSI deep pullback (RSI<30) on ETH/1h with 8:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 12.0x ATR TP). Uses EMA_200 for trend confirmation and EMA_50 slope for momentum. The deep-dip variant enters ONLY when RSI reaches truly oversold levels within a confirmed uptrend — targeting liquidation cascade bottoms. Compared to the standard RSI<40 entry (161 trades, PF=1.71), this should produce fewer but higher-quality entries with larger average winners due to greater displacement from the trend.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 confirms long-term uptrend is intact.
EMA_50 slope is positive (current bar EMA_50 > previous bar EMA_50).
RSI_14 <= 30.0 (deeply oversold — liquidation cascade or panic selling in uptrend).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms long-term downtrend is intact.
EMA_50 slope is negative (current bar EMA_50 < previous bar EMA_50).
RSI_14 >= 70.0 (deeply overbought — euphoria exhaustion in downtrend).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
RSI long threshold: 30 (deeply oversold — stricter than default 40)
RSI short threshold: 70 (deeply overbought — stricter than default 60)
Regime gate: ADX_14 used for regime classification (trending>25, ranging<20, transitional 20-25)
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure: if trades < 40, RSI<30 is too restrictive for 1h on ETH
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — long-term trend intact, medium-term momentum positive
RSI_14 <= 30 (deeply oversold) — targeting liquidation cascade bottoms in uptrend
ATR_14 for stop/TP — 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP for 8:1 R:R

variants.name: rsi_deep_pullback_1h_9to1
variants.description: RSI deep pullback (RSI<30) on ETH/1h with wider 9:1 R:R (1.5x ATR stop, 13.5x ATR TP). Same deep-dip entry as the 8:1 variant but with wider TP to capture more of the recovery move. At deep oversold entries, the bounce-back distance from RSI<30 to RSI=50 (fair value) is typically LARGER than from RSI<40 to RSI=50. The wider TP capitalizes on this — the hypothesis is that deep dips recover more violently, meaning the extra 1x ATR of TP distance is achievable without meaningfully reducing win rate.
entry_long: Close > EMA_200 confirms long-term uptrend is intact.
EMA_50 slope is positive (current bar EMA_50 > previous bar EMA_50).
entry_short: Close < EMA_200 confirms long-term downtrend is intact.
EMA_50 slope is negative (current bar EMA_50 < previous bar EMA_50).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Regime gate: ADX_14 used for regime classification
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (13.5x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure: if win rate < 10% vs 8:1 variant's win rate, the extra 1.5x ATR TP is unreachable
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 13.5x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_200 trend + EMA_50 slope — same baseline as 8:1 variant
RSI_14 <= 30 — deep pullback, paired with wider 9:1 TP to capture fuller recovery
ATR_14 for stop/TP — 1.5x stop / 13.5x TP for 9:1 R:R

variants.name: rsi_deep_pullback_fast_trend_1h_8to1
variants.description: RSI deep pullback (RSI<30) on ETH/1h with 8:1 R:R but using EMA_50 as trend EMA (instead of EMA_200) and EMA_21 as slope EMA. This is the FAST-TREND variant. EMA_200 requires a long-established trend — on 1h, that's ~8 days of consistent direction. EMA_50 only needs ~2 days. The fast-trend approach captures shorter-duration trends that EMA_200 would miss entirely. The tradeoff: more trades (shorter trends = more opportunities) but potentially more whipsaws (shorter trends are less reliable). With RSI<30 as the strict dip filter, we still require genuine oversold conditions — the fast trend just expands the UNIVERSE of trends we trade in, not the quality bar for entry.
entry_long: Close > EMA_50 confirms medium-term uptrend (faster trend detection than EMA_200).
EMA_21 slope is positive (current bar EMA_21 > previous bar EMA_21 — fast momentum).
RSI_14 <= 30.0 (deeply oversold — same strict dip filter).
entry_short: EMA_21 slope is negative (current bar EMA_21 < previous bar EMA_21).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
Trend EMA: EMA_50 (fast trend — ~2 days on 1h)
Regime gate: ADX_14 used for regime classification
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.5x ATR).
Hypothesis failure: if PF < standard EMA_200 variant, fast trend detection introduces whipsaw that degrades edge
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.5x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 fast trend + EMA_21 fast slope — captures shorter-duration trends that EMA_200 misses
RSI_14 <= 30 — same deep pullback filter, but in context of faster trends
ATR_14 for stop/TP — 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP for 8:1 R:R
